{{Quote|General Geen is an invaluable contact for networking with other resources. While he cannot provide direct support, he often has friends who are more than willing to supply information, materiel or assistance to friends of the Alliance|[[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]]|<ref name="CRO" />}}
'''Locus Geen''' was a [[General/Legends|General]] in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]], who fought with distinction in the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. He later became an informant for the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. Around [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] he established himself in the upper echelons of society on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Salliche/Legends|Salliche]] and passed along sensitive information to the Rebels.

==Biography==
Following in the footsteps of his forefathers, Geen served during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] as the commanding officer of [[Rom Mohc]].<ref name="DF Saga">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050407a|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 1}}</ref> After the war, Geen retired to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Salliche/Legends|Salliche]], where he became involved in the planet's bureaucracy. He cultivated contacts among Salliche's senators and bureaucrats, Imperial naval officers, and acquaintances from the Clone Wars. As a war hero he was invited to high-level receptions on Salliche where he would entertain by telling exaggerated stories from the Clone Wars.<ref name="CRO" />

He spent his time between his estate on Salliche and various plazas and forums in the capital city, [[Netassa]] where he would argue politics with legislators and entertain younger generations with his war stories.<ref name="CRO" />

==Personality and traits==
Geen was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]]. By [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] he was mostly bald but had a tidy beard and he was hunched over. He was known as an entertaining storyteller, often telling stories of the ancient wars whenever possible. He was also a good listener and gleaned information from his contacts, which he passed on to the Rebellion through encrypted communications to sympathizers. He was generally contacted through [[Imperial NetPost]], and contacts seeking him would claim to be relatives of "old war friends."<ref name="CRO" />

==Skills and abilities==
In his youth Geen was skilled at combat and was a solid commander. In his later years he became very involved with local government and bureaucracy and could move skillfully around upper echelons of society.<ref name="CRO" />

==Equipment==
By 0 ABY Geen used a cane to help him walk. He also carried a [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]] that was linked up to the Imperial NetPost so he could monitor requests from the Rebellion.<ref name="CRO" />

==Behind the scenes==
Locus Geen was designed by [[Peter M. Schweighofer]] for ''[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]'', a supplement for the ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' that was published by [[West End Games]] in [[1994]]. He was illustrated by [[Mike Vilardi]].
